sections: When a Man and Van Beats a Full Removal

The skill in choosing a man and van service lies in matching the job to the right level of resource. After years of moving families and businesses across Sussex, we’ve learned that a single van and a capable mover is the smart, cost-effective choice for a clearly defined set of jobs — and an unnecessary expense for others. A man and van works brilliantly when the volume is modest and the route is straightforward: a one or two-bedroom flat with no excess furniture, a handful of boxes and a sofa, a few pieces of large furniture being relocated, or a part-load that doesn’t justify a full crew. If you can picture everything fitting comfortably in one Luton van with room to spare, a man and van is almost always the leaner option.

Where it stops being the right call is when the property is substantial, the access is awkward over multiple floors, or the timeline is tight enough that one or two people simply can’t shift everything inside the day. A three or four-bedroom house packed to capacity, or a home that needs careful dismantling and wrapping of antique and high-value pieces, is better served by our full house removals service, where a larger team and bigger vehicle keep the day on schedule. We’ll always give you an honest steer at the quote stage — if a man and van genuinely won’t cut it, we’d rather tell you upfront than leave you short on the day.

Some of our busiest man and van work involves moving just one or two things — and these jobs deserve as much care as a full house move. We regularly collect single items such as a wardrobe, a dining table, a washing machine or a three-seater sofa, and we handle marketplace and online purchases that need a careful pair of hands to get them home in one piece. If you’ve bought a piece of furniture on a buy-and-sell site, won an auction lot, or are sending an item to a relative across the county, a man and van is purpose-built for the task.

We bring the equipment that makes these collections painless rather than a wrestling match, including straps, blankets, trolleys and protective wrap. Our movers know how to read a piece before lifting it — checking whether a leg unscrews, whether a door panel needs taping, or whether a glass shelf travels safer in the cab. Typical single-item and part-load jobs we cover include:

  • Marketplace and auction collections from a seller’s address to yours
  • Furniture deliveries and one-off drop-offs across Sussex and the bordering counties
  • Appliance moves — fridges, freezers, washing machines and tumble dryers
  • Part-loads where you only need a portion of a van, not the whole vehicle
  • Transporting a single bulky purchase that won’t fit in a car

Student life means moving often and travelling light, which makes a man and van the natural fit. End-of-term clear-outs, the dash between halls and a shared house, or relocating from a studio into your first proper flat all tend to come down to a few boxes, a desk, a mattress and the contents of a wardrobe — exactly the kind of load one van handles in a single, well-planned run. We’re used to the realities of student timetables and tight tenancy hand-overs, and we work quickly without ever treating your belongings carelessly. If you’re heading to or from a campus in the region, our dedicated student removals service sits alongside our man and van work to keep costs down for those on a budget.

Small flat relocations follow the same logic. A first move out of a parental home, a downsize after the children have left, or shifting between two rented flats rarely needs a full removal team. We’ll bring the van, do the lifting and loading, and keep the day moving so you’re settled in your new place without a long, draining wait. For couples and individuals across West Sussex and the surrounding area, a man and van strikes the right balance between affordability and a properly professional service.
What’s Included — Loading Help, Care & Protection

Our man and van service is more than a driver and a vehicle. Every booking comes with experienced loading help, because how a van is packed determines whether everything arrives undamaged. Our movers stack heavy items low, secure them with ratchet straps against the van rails, and slot fragile or oddly shaped pieces where they won’t shift in transit. We carry moving blankets and protective wrap as standard, so furniture is shielded from knocks against doorframes, walls and other items on the journey.

As a family-run Sussex company, we treat your belongings the way we’d treat our own. We’re fully insured with £10m public liability cover, a LAPADA member and Checkatrade-verified, and we’re recommended by respected local agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart — the kind of trust that doesn’t come from cutting corners. A standard man and van booking includes:

  • A clean, well-maintained van sized to your load
  • One or more uniformed movers to handle the lifting and carrying
  • Blankets, straps and protective wrapping for furniture
  • Careful loading and secure stacking so nothing shifts in transit
  • Full insurance cover for genuine peace of mind

If your move also needs boxes and protection sorted before collection day, our full packing service can take that off your hands too.

The smoothest man and van jobs are the ones where we’ve thought about access before we arrive. Tight lanes, controlled parking zones, narrow stairwells, lifts with size limits and timed loading bays all affect how long a job takes and where the van can sit. When you book, let us know about anything unusual at either end — a third-floor flat with no lift, a permit-only street, a shared driveway or a property set well back from the road — and we’ll plan the day around it. If a parking suspension or permit is needed, it’s worth arranging in advance so we’re not circling the block on the day.

Booking a slot is straightforward. Tell us what you’re moving, the two addresses, and your preferred date, and we’ll confirm a time that works. Because we operate across West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent from our base at Doryln House in Ashington, near Pulborough, we can usually fit local man and van jobs in at short notice. To get started, request a no-obligation quote, review our straightforward pricing, or call the team on 01903 893731. You can also email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk and we’ll come straight back to you.
Clearing a Few Rooms & Bridging Moves

A man and van is ideal when you need to clear just part of a property rather than the whole thing — a spare room, a garage, or the contents of a flat being readied for sale or new tenants. We can move what you’re keeping to a new home or into secure storage, and where a property needs fully emptying of unwanted items, our separate house clearance service handles the heavier, end-to-end work. The two pair up neatly: a man and van shifts the pieces you’re holding on to, while clearance deals with the rest.

Bridging moves are another common use — the in-between trips that crop up when completion dates don’t line up, when you’re moving belongings ahead of the main move, or when furniture needs relocating into storage for a few weeks. As a long-established local firm that has worked in this area since 2016, we know the routes, the awkward streets and the realities of Sussex moving days. Whatever the scale, you can read more about our family-run team and the care we bring to every job, large or small.
faqs: What size loads can a man and van handle?

A single man and van comfortably handles anything from one item up to roughly a one or two-bedroom flat’s worth of furniture and boxes, depending on access. If your move is bigger than that — a busy three or four-bedroom house, for example — we’ll usually recommend our full house removals service with a larger team and vehicle so everything is moved safely within the day.
Do you help with the lifting and loading, or just drive?

We do far more than drive. Every man and van booking includes hands-on loading help from experienced movers who carry, wrap and securely stack your belongings. We bring blankets, straps and trolleys as standard, so heavy and awkward items are handled properly rather than left for you to wrestle with.
Can you collect a single item or a marketplace purchase?

Yes — single-item collections are one of our most common jobs. Whether it’s a sofa, a wardrobe, an appliance or something you’ve bought on a buy-and-sell or auction site, we’ll collect it from the seller and deliver it to you across Sussex and the neighbouring counties, fully wrapped and insured for the journey.
How do I book, and how much notice do you need?

Booking is simple: tell us what you’re moving, the two addresses and your preferred date through our quote form, or call 01903 893731. For local man and van work we can often accommodate short-notice slots, though booking ahead helps secure your ideal time — especially around busy weekends and month-end completion dates.
